ddls:
CREATE TABLE "lift_usage" (
  "usage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each usage event */,
  "time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the lift usage event */,
  "chair" TEXT /* Name of the chairlift used */,
  "vertical_feet" INTEGER /* Vertical distance ascended in feet */,
  "vertical_meters" INTEGER /* Vertical distance ascended in meters */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the usage event */,
  "month" INTEGER /* Month of the usage event */,
  "day" INTEGER /* Day of the usage event */,
  "time_of_day" TEXT /* Time of day when the lift was used */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the usage event */,
  "pass_id" TEXT /* Identifier for the ski pass used */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* User ID linked to the usage event */,
  "weather_conditions" TEXT /* Weather conditions during the lift usage */,
  "lift_status" TEXT /* Operational status of the lift at the time of usage */,
  "season" TEXT /* Ski season of the usage event */,
  "lift_type" TEXT /* Type of lift used (e.g., chairlift, gondola, magic carpet) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("usage_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for logging into the system */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for security */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Role ID linked to the roles table */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address for contact */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number for contact */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role and its responsibilities */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "chairlifts" (
  "chair_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each chairlift */,
  "chair_name" TEXT /* Name of the chairlift */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the chairlift on the resort */,
  "capacity" INTEGER /* Maximum number of guests the chairlift can carry at once */,
  "length" INTEGER /* Length of the chairlift in meters */,
  "vertical_ascend" INTEGER /* Maximum vertical distance the chairlift can ascend in meters */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current operational status of the chairlift */,
  "last_maintenance" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance check */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("chair_id")
)
